skills.title: PCB design (walking skeleton — discovery build)
skills.summary: Walking-skeleton PCB editor. Opens .pcbskel board documents (JSON), renders a crude component view, and supports platform pcb_route_hint annotations with sidecar persistence. Backend serves minerva_pcb_ping plus echo handlers for pcb.serialize/deserialize/collect_export/apply_export. The real editor canvas, Python worker, and full 31-tool surface land in later rounds; .minpcb stays with the in-tree editor until then.
skills.preconditions: A .pcbskel document is open OR the user has asked for PCB work (an anonymous editor can be spawned via minerva_create_plugin_editor with editor_item_id "new_pcb").
skills.outcome: A .pcbskel board renders in the panel; pcb_route_hint annotations can be authored via the annotation dock, persist to the <file>.annotations.json sidecar, and are readable via minerva_annotations_query / minerva_annotations_render_overlay.
skills.steps:
1. Call minerva_pcb_ping to confirm the backend is alive.
2. Open a .pcbskel file (or create one via editor_item new_pcb).
3. Use the core minerva_annotations_* tools against the panel's editor tab for route-hint work.
4. For anything beyond the skeleton slice (real geometry, traces, export), explain that it lands in later rounds — the in-tree PCB editor still owns .minpcb.
skills.system_prompt: The PCB plugin is a walking skeleton (discovery build). It opens .pcbskel JSON boards, draws components crudely, and supports pcb_route_hint annotations through the platform annotation substrate (dock authoring, sidecar persistence, minerva_annotations_query/render_overlay). Backend tools: minerva_pcb_ping plus echo handlers for the four pcb.* save channels. There is NO geometry engine, worker, or trace routing yet; .minpcb documents still open in the in-tree PCB editor, not this plugin.
